coverage.note: No production code changed — this session only added/modified PLang .goal test files

findings.issue: Builder misaligned step indices — from step 15 onward, each step's action belongs to the NEXT step. Step 15 text is 'assert %partsCount% equals 3' but action is list.get. The shifted sequence happens to be functionally equivalent so tests pass, but assertions don't verify what the step text claims.
findings.impact: The assert for partsCount==3 never runs. If the list had wrong count, the test would still pass. Sort/reverse/unique assertions are also shifted — weaker coverage than intended.
findings.suggestion: Builder system prompt issue. The LLM returns off-by-one indices when appending new steps to an existing goal. On re-build, it approves the misaligned data without catching the text/action mismatch. Need to adjust builder prompt to verify step text matches action semantics during review.

findings.issue: Builder hardcodes condition parameter as literal true/false instead of the expression '%x% == 10'. Also, goalIfFalse parameter has type 'goal.call' with no value, which the runtime interprets as calling a goal with empty name — causing 'Goal not found' error.
findings.impact: Condition module has zero PLang test coverage. The if/else branch logic is not tested at all.
findings.suggestion: Builder system prompt issue. The condition.if action expects a bool expression that evaluates at runtime, not a pre-evaluated literal. The LLM needs guidance that %variable% comparisons should remain as expressions. Also, optional goal.call params should be omitted entirely (not included with empty value).

findings.issue: Pre-existing test failure — 'something went wrong' error. Not related to this session's changes.
findings.impact: ErrorHandling module test coverage is broken. Known issue from v1.
findings.suggestion: Investigate separately — this predates the current work.

summary: Created 4 new PLang test suites (File, Output, Assert, Condition) and extended 3 existing (ListOps, Math, Mock). 20/22 PLang tests pass, 1423/1423 C# tests pass. Two builder issues block full coverage: (1) condition.if gets hardcoded literals instead of runtime expressions, (2) ListOps new steps have off-by-one action alignment causing false green. Both are builder system prompt issues, not test logic errors. File, Output, Assert, Math extensions, and Mock extensions all work correctly.
